body.title: /export-chat skill — dump today's CC chat log to /home/akien/TheIgors/claude_chat_logs/YYYYMMDD.md
body.result: lab/claudecode/export_chat.py renders transcripts to claude_chat_logs/YYYY-MM-DD.md. Supports --all (bulk), --session <id>, --dry-run. Elides tool results to 200 chars for readability. SKILL.md wired up. Output verified: 2026-04-20.md written (926KB).
body.description:
Akien: 'if something scrolls off the top, that gives me a way to recover it.'

## Flow
- Skill reads the current conversation transcript (from CC's transcript file, likely ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl)
- Renders to readable markdown (user turns, assistant turns, tool calls elided or summarized)
- Writes/overwrites /home/akien/TheIgors/claude_chat_logs/YYYYMMDD.md (or appends if session crosses midnight)
- Prints path + bytes written

## On-demand only
No auto-run. Akien invokes when he wants a snapshot.

## Not in scope
- Backfilling older chat logs from scattered sources — separate ticket T-chat-history-igor-backfill

## Implementation notes
- CC session transcript path: ~/.claude/projects/-home-akien-TheIgors/<session-id>.jsonl (JSONL, one message per line)
- Needs to pick the current session — the session-id visible in hook context, or the most recent file by mtime in that dir
- Render: parse each line, format user/assistant turns, strip internal tool-call noise but keep tool-result summaries
